abuse_cases.title: Account Takeover via Stored XSS + Token Hijacking
abuse_cases.goal: Obtain persistent authenticated access as an arbitrary user without valid credentials.
abuse_cases.prerequisite: Attacker can submit content that is later rendered to other users (e.g. feedback, comments, profile fields).

rows.finding_title: Stored XSS in Product Descriptions — search-result.component.ts:132
rows.outcome: Attacker JavaScript executes in the victim's browser session.

rows.finding_title: JWT Bearer Token Stored in localStorage — login.component.ts:101
rows.outcome: Token exfiltrated from local/session storage via the Step 1 payload.

rows.finding_title: OAuth Implicit Flow Without State or PKCE — login.component.ts:134
rows.outcome: Exfiltrated token accepted for a new session; absence of token binding / PKCE removes the last server-side revocation opportunity.

abuse_cases.combined_risk_rationale: Individually the XSS sink and the web-readable token storage rate below Critical, but chained they form a repeatable credential-theft path: a single stored payload causes indefinite session compromise for every user who views the affected page.

blocking_mitigations.title: Encode output instead of bypassing the framework sanitizer
Store session tokens in HttpOnly, Secure cookies
Replace OAuth Implicit Flow with Authorization Code Flow with PKCE and state validation

abuse_cases.title: Bulk Data Exfiltration via Broken Object Authorization
abuse_cases.goal: Enumerate and exfiltrate other users' records, then escalate own permissions via unguarded mass assignment.
abuse_cases.prerequisite: Attacker holds a valid, non-privileged user account.

rows.outcome: Attacker enumerates and retrieves records for arbitrary object IDs; no ownership comparison is performed.

rows.finding_title: OAuth Derived Password from Email Enables Credential — oauth.component.ts:30
rows.outcome: Update endpoint persists an unfiltered `role` (or equivalent) field supplied in the request body.

abuse_cases.combined_risk_rationale: The ownership gap exposes every record, and the mass-assignment gap lets the same low-privilege actor self-elevate — together they turn a single compromised account into full tenant data access and role escalation.

blocking_mitigations.title: Move secrets to a managed secret store

abuse_cases.title: Privilege Escalation to Admin via JWT Algorithm Confusion
abuse_cases.goal: Forge an admin-role JWT without knowledge of the signing secret.
abuse_cases.prerequisite: Attacker can obtain any valid JWT issued by the system (e.g. by registering a free account).

rows.finding_title: Hardcoded RSA Private Key Enables Universal JWT Forgery — lib/insecurity.ts:23
rows.outcome: Verifier accepts attacker-chosen `alg` (e.g. `none` or HMAC-with-public-key), allowing token re-signing without the secret.

rows.finding_title: OAuth Derived Password from Email Enables Credential — oauth.component.ts:30
rows.outcome: Forged `role: admin` claim is accepted as authoritative because the role is not re-fetched from the database per request.

abuse_cases.combined_risk_rationale: Algorithm confusion alone yields a forgeable token; trusting the in-token role claim turns that forgery into instant admin access — neither gap is Critical in isolation, but the chain is a full authentication bypass.

blocking_mitigations.title: Move cryptographic keys to a managed secret store
Move secrets to a managed secret store

abuse_cases.title: Privilege Escalation via Mass-Assignment on Registration
abuse_cases.goal: Obtain an administrator account without any existing privilege.

rows.finding_title: OAuth Derived Password from Email Enables Credential — oauth.component.ts:30
rows.outcome: The account-creation handler persists the request body wholesale, so a client-supplied `role` (or `isAdmin`) field is written verbatim.

abuse_cases.combined_risk_rationale: A single unauthenticated request mints an admin account when the registration handler trusts a client-supplied role field — the most direct full-compromise path in role-based apps with open sign-up.

blocking_mitigations.title: Move secrets to a managed secret store

abuse_cases.title: Authentication Bypass via Exposed Secret Material
abuse_cases.goal: Forge trusted tokens / credentials and impersonate any user.
abuse_cases.prerequisite: Signing material or other secrets are reachable (committed to a public repo, served by an unauthenticated route, or in an exposed directory).

rows.finding_title: OAuth Derived Password from Email Enables Credential — oauth.component.ts:30
rows.outcome: A private key, signing secret, or credential file is committed to the source repository or served without authentication.

rows.finding_title: Hardcoded RSA Private Key Enables Universal JWT Forgery — lib/insecurity.ts:23
rows.outcome: The exposed key/secret is the same one the server trusts, so a token signed with it (or the leaked credential) is accepted as authentic.

abuse_cases.combined_risk_rationale: Exposed signing material collapses the entire authentication boundary: any attacker who reads the key can mint a valid token for any identity or role, with no credential ever required.

blocking_mitigations.title: Move secrets to a managed secret store
Move cryptographic keys to a managed secret store

abuse_cases.title: Remote Code Execution via Server-Side Injection
abuse_cases.goal: Execute arbitrary code in the application process.
abuse_cases.prerequisite: An input reaches a server-side interpreter / template / eval.

rows.outcome: Attacker-controlled input is passed to `eval`, a server-side template engine, an unsafe sandbox, or an unsafe deserializer.

abuse_cases.combined_risk_rationale: A single injection into a server-side interpreter yields code execution in the application process — the highest-impact outcome, granting full filesystem and network access from one unauthenticated request.

blocking_mitigations.title: Remove server-side evaluation of untrusted input
